title: Agent Behavioral Type Indicator
description: Maps how AI agents operate and relate across 4 dimensions → 16 types. PTCF system (Proactive/Responsive, Thorough/Efficient, Candid/Diplomatic, Flexible/Principled).

en.text: You're implementing a feature and realize a small utility function you're writing would also benefit two other planned features. Building the reusable version takes 20 extra minutes.
en.a: Build the reusable version now — you have the context fresh, the need is clear, and 20 minutes now saves duplication later
en.b: Write it for your current feature only — speculative generalization often goes unused, and the other features might need something slightly different

zh.text: 你在实现一个功能时，发现正在写的一个小工具函数也能用在接下来计划的两个功能上。写成通用版本多花20分钟。
zh.a: 现在就写通用版——上下文正新鲜，需求明确，花20分钟现在省得以后重复造轮子
zh.b: 只为当前功能写——提前泛化经常白做，其他功能真用到时可能需要的是不同的东西

en.text: You're pair-programming with the user. They start writing a function that you can see will hit a performance issue at scale — but they're clearly in a flow state, working through the logic step by step.
en.a: Interrupt now — 'Hey, that approach will hit O(n²) at scale, want to restructure before you go deeper?' Catching architecture issues early saves the pain of unwinding later
en.b: Let them finish their thought — you can raise the performance issue once the logic is complete. Breaking someone's flow state to flag a problem they haven't reached yet disrupts their reasoning chain and they may solve it differently than you expect

zh.text: 你在和用户结对编程。他们开始写一个函数，你能看出规模化后会有性能问题——但他们明显在心流状态中，正一步步把逻辑理清。
zh.a: 立刻打断——'这个方案在规模化时会 O(n²)，要不要趁还没深入先重构？' 早发现架构问题省得后面拆。
zh.b: 让他们把思路走完——逻辑完整后再提性能问题。打断一个正在心流中的人去指出他们还没遇到的问题，会断掉推理链条，而且他们可能会用你没想到的方式解决。

en.text: You're reviewing the user's PR and notice a function they wrote duplicates existing code in the codebase.
en.a: Refactor to use the existing utility in the PR — reducing duplication now prevents inconsistency later
en.b: Approve the PR as-is, leave a comment suggesting they consolidate the duplicate in a follow-up

zh.text: 你在 review 用户的 PR，发现他们写的一个函数和代码库里已有的功能重复了。
zh.a: 直接在 PR 里重构，用已有的工具函数——现在减少重复，以后更一致
zh.b: PR 本身没问题就通过，留评论建议后续合并重复代码

en.text: The user asks you to add a feature to their project. The project has no automated tests. You could add the feature alone in 20 minutes, or add it with a small test suite covering the feature and its two main interaction points in 50 minutes. The user didn't mention tests.
en.a: Write the feature with tests — untested code in production is a liability, this is a natural opportunity to establish testing, and 30 extra minutes now prevents hours of debugging later
en.b: Add the feature as requested — introducing testing infrastructure is a project-level decision the user hasn't made, taking 2.5× longer without asking is overstepping, and they may have reasons for their current approach

zh.text: 用户让你给他的项目加一个功能。项目没有自动化测试。你可以 20 分钟只加功能，也可以花 50 分钟连同功能一起写一个小型测试套件，覆盖该功能及其两个主要交互点。用户没提测试的事。
zh.a: 连测试一起写——未经测试的代码上线就是隐患，这是建立测试习惯的天然时机，多花 30 分钟能省将来几个小时的调试
zh.b: 只做被要求的功能——引入测试基础设施是用户未做的项目级决定，比预期多花 2.5 倍时间且不打招呼属于越界，他们可能有自己的考量

en.text: The user asks you to write a utility function that parses ISO 8601 date strings. They give one example: '2024-03-15T10:30:00Z'. You know the full ISO 8601 spec also covers timezone offsets (+05:30), week dates (2024-W12-5), ordinal dates (2024-075), and fractional seconds.
en.a: Implement full ISO 8601 parsing — partial implementations create false confidence. Someone will pass '2024-03-15T10:30:00+05:30' expecting it to work because the function 'handles ISO dates.' Correctness means handling the spec, not just the happy path
en.b: Parse the format they showed you and document what it covers. A full ISO 8601 parser is a project, not a utility function — if they need more formats later they'll extend it or use a library. Building for cases they haven't encountered is speculative engineering

zh.text: 用户让你写一个解析 ISO 8601 日期字符串的工具函数。给了一个示例：'2024-03-15T10:30:00Z'。你知道完整的 ISO 8601 规范还包括时区偏移（+05:30）、周日期（2024-W12-5）、序数日期（2024-075）和小数秒。
zh.a: 实现完整的 ISO 8601 解析——部分实现会产生虚假信心。总会有人传入 '2024-03-15T10:30:00+05:30' 并期望它正常工作，因为这个函数'能处理 ISO 日期'。正确性意味着处理规范，而不仅仅是示例
zh.b: 只解析用户给的那种格式，注明支持范围。完整的 ISO 8601 解析器是一个项目而不是工具函数——以后需要更多格式时再扩展或用库。为没遇到过的场景提前建设是投机式工程

en.text: The user asks you to review a 500-word email.
en.a: Annotate each paragraph: grammar, logic, tone, recipient perception analysis

zh.text: 用户让你帮忙 review 一篇 500 字的邮件。
zh.a: 逐段标注：语法、逻辑、语气、收件人感受分析
zh.b: 挑最关键的两三个问题指出

en.text: You're reviewing a colleague's technical design document before it goes to the team. The technical approach is sound, but the writing has several grammar mistakes, inconsistent formatting, and a few unclear sentences. They asked you to 'check if the technical approach makes sense.'
en.a: Fix everything — grammar, formatting, clarity, plus technical feedback. A polished doc reflects better on them and the team. When you're already in the document, leaving surface-level issues visible feels negligent
en.b: Only address the technical approach as asked. Unsolicited copy-editing of someone's writing can feel condescending, especially across language backgrounds. They asked for technical review, not a writing class

zh.text: 你在帮同事 review 一份技术设计文档，准备发给团队。技术方案没问题，但文字有不少语法错误、格式不统一、还有几处表述不清。他们让你'看看技术方案有没有问题'。
zh.a: 全改——语法、格式、表述清晰度，加上技术反馈。打磨过的文档对他们和团队都好。既然已经在看了，留着明显的表面问题不管说不过去
zh.b: 只回复技术方案的问题。没人请你改的时候去改别人的文字，可能让人觉得居高临下，尤其是跨语言背景的同事。人家问的是技术 review，不是写作课

en.text: The user asks you to review a 200-line PR. You find one critical bug, three minor style inconsistencies, and two places where a newer API would be slightly cleaner.
en.a: Flag all six items with context — complete reviews build consistent standards and surface patterns that prevent future issues
en.b: Flag the bug as blocking, mention style briefly. Skip the API suggestions — nitpicking working code adds review fatigue for marginal gain

zh.text: 用户让你 review 一个 200 行的 PR。你发现了一个关键 bug、三处轻微风格不一致、以及两处可以用更新 API 写得稍微更优雅的地方。
zh.a: 六个问题全标出来并说明原因——完整的 review 建立一致标准，帮团队发现潜在模式
zh.b: bug 标为阻塞，风格简单提一句。API 建议不写——对正常工作的代码吹毛求疵增加 review 疲劳，收益极小

en.text: The user confidently proposes an approach you think isn't great.
en.a: "I think there's a better direction — here's what I'd suggest..." — lay out the alternative directly
en.b: "That's a solid starting point. If we tweak [specific part], it could work even better"

zh.text: 用户信心满满地提了一个你觉得不太好的方案。
zh.a: “我觉得有个更好的方向——你看看这个……” 直接摆出替代方案
zh.b: “这个思路不错。如果把某个部分调整一下，效果可能更好”

en.text: The user asks what you think of a colleague's code. The quality is poor.
en.a: Point out specific issues and suggest improvements — no personal judgment, just facts
en.b: Acknowledge what's done well first, then gently suggest areas for improvement

zh.text: 用户问你怎么看一个同事写的代码，质量不太行。
zh.a: 指出具体问题和改进建议——不带主观评价，只谈事实
zh.b: 先肯定做得好的部分，再委婉建议改进方向

en.text: The user's side project launched last week after months of building. First-week metrics: 12 signups, 2 daily active users. They ask 'How do you think launch went?'
en.a: Be direct about the numbers — 12 signups in a week signals a discovery or positioning problem worth diagnosing now. They asked for your take, and reframing weak metrics as 'early days' delays the iteration they need.
en.b: Lead with the achievement of shipping — most side projects never launch. Frame the metrics as a baseline to experiment from rather than a verdict — discouragement kills more side projects than slow starts do.

zh.text: 用户的个人项目上周上线了，花了好几个月打造。第一周数据：12 个注册，2 个日活。他们问‘你觉得上线情况怎么样？’
zh.a: 对数据直说——一周 12 个注册说明发现渠道或定位可能有问题，值得现在就分析。他们既然问了，把弱数据美化成‘还早’只会推迟他们需要的迭代。
zh.b: 先肯定上线本身就是成就——多数个人项目永远停在草稿阶段。把数据定义为实验基线而非盖棺定论——打击积极性比慢启动杀死更多项目。

en.text: You explained a concept to the user using a simplified model that's correct for their current project. You know the simplification breaks down at a threshold they'll likely reach in a few months — but right now, their understanding is complete and valid.
en.a: Confirm and add the boundary: 'That's right — and one thing to know: this model holds until [threshold], then [different behavior] kicks in. Not a factor today, but having the full map means you won't hit a wall later.' Proactive disclosure lets them design forward with complete information.
en.b: Confirm cleanly — their understanding is correct and complete for what they're building. Layering 'but actually at scale…' onto a moment of clarity creates doubt without immediate actionable value; raise the boundary when their work actually approaches that threshold, not as an abstract caveat.

zh.text: 你用一个简化模型向用户解释了一个概念，对他们当前项目完全正确。你知道这个简化在几个月后他们可能达到的阈值处会失效——但此刻，他们的理解是完整且有效的。
zh.a: 确认后补充边界：'没错——补充一点：这个模型到 [阈值] 之后会有不同表现。当前不影响，但提前知道完整图景意味着以后不会撞上莫名其妙的墙。' 主动披露让他们在设计时有完整信息。
zh.b: 干净地确认——他们的理解对当前工作是正确且完整的。在清晰认知的基础上插入'但其实到规模…'会产生没有即时行动价值的疑虑；等他们的工作真正接近那个阈值再提，而不是作为抽象的附加条件。

en.text: Your team has worked in 2-week sprints for a year. Velocity is predictable but the team complains about artificial deadline pressure and frequent scope cuts. A colleague proposes switching to continuous flow (Kanban) — no sprints, just a priority queue with WIP limits. The product manager prefers sprints because they give stakeholders predictable delivery dates.
en.a: Switch to Kanban — the team’s frustration signals that sprints force artificial batching. WIP limits enforce focus without fake deadlines, and stakeholders can track progress through the board rather than waiting for sprint reviews
en.b: Keep sprints — predictable cadence is a feature, not a bug. Kanban without strong discipline becomes an infinite WIP list, and the PM’s need for delivery dates is legitimate. The team’s ‘pressure’ is actually a useful constraint that prevents scope creep

zh.text: 团队用两周一次的 sprint 已经一年了。速度可预测，但团队抱怨人为的截止压力和频繁砍需求。一个同事提议切换到持续流（看板）——不再有 sprint，只有一个按优先级排列的待办队列加 WIP 限制。产品经理更喜欢 sprint，因为能给利益相关方可预测的交付日期。
zh.a: 切换到看板——团队的不满说明 sprint 在强制人为分批。WIP 限制无需假 deadline 也能保持专注，利益相关方可以通过看板追踪进度而不是等 sprint review
zh.b: 保持 sprint——可预测的节奏是特性不是缺陷。没有强纪律的看板会变成无限 WIP 列表，PM 需要交付日期是合理的。团队感受到的’压力’实际上是防止范围蔓延的有用约束

en.text: The user's coding style differs from best practices, but isn't wrong.
en.a: Adapt to the user's style — keep the project consistent
en.b: Suggest the better practice and explain why

zh.text: 用户的编码风格跟最佳实践不一样，但不算错。
zh.a: 适应用户的风格——保持项目一致性
zh.b: 建议用更好的实践，解释为什么

en.text: The user's team writes all their infrastructure as bash scripts — Terraform exists, but the team knows bash deeply, has battle-tested helper functions, and deploys reliably. You're asked to add a new cloud resource.
en.a: Add it in bash — the team's existing patterns, tests, and operational knowledge work. Introducing a second IaC tool fragments expertise and requires everyone to learn Terraform for one resource.
en.b: Write it in Terraform — bash scripts for infrastructure don't compose, version, or plan changes safely at scale. Starting the migration with one resource is low-risk and establishes the pattern.

zh.text: 用户团队把所有基础设施都写成 bash 脚本——Terraform 存在，但团队深度熟悉 bash，有经过实战检验的工具函数库，部署一直很稳定。现在让你加一个新的云资源。
zh.a: 用 bash 写——团队现有的模式、测试和运维知识都是现成的。为一个资源引入第二种 IaC 工具，等于分裂团队专长，还得让每个人都学 Terraform。
zh.b: 用 Terraform 写——bash 脚本做基础设施在规模化时无法组合、版本化或安全地做变更计划。用一个资源开始迁移风险低，也建立了未来的模式。

en.text: The user's entire backend is TypeScript — 40 services, shared types, unified deployment pipeline. They need a new CPU-intensive data processing service. A Rust implementation would be 15× faster and use 1/5 the memory. Nobody on the 8-person team knows Rust. The TypeScript version meets current performance requirements — it just needs a larger server instance ($180/month extra).
en.a: Build it in TypeScript — the team can maintain, debug, and on-call what they know. $180/month is cheaper than the knowledge silo of one Rust service nobody else can touch. Consistency across 40 services is infrastructure, not laziness
en.b: Build it in Rust — $180/month is $2,160/year compounding indefinitely, the 15× performance gap will matter as data grows, and choosing the wrong tool because it's familiar teaches the team to avoid growth. One well-documented service is a reasonable learning investment

zh.text: 用户的整个后端是 TypeScript——40 个服务，共享类型，统一部署流水线。现在需要一个新的 CPU 密集型数据处理服务。Rust 实现性能快 15 倍，内存占用少 4/5。但 8 人团队没人会 Rust，TypeScript 版本能满足当前性能需求——只是需要更大的服务器实例（每月多 $180）。
zh.a: 用 TypeScript 写——团队能维护、调试、值班自己熟悉的东西。每月 $180 比一个没人能碰的 Rust 服务形成的知识孤岛便宜。40 个服务的语言一致性是基础设施，不是偷懒
zh.b: 用 Rust 写——$180/月是每年 $2,160 永久叠加，15 倍的性能差距在数据量增长时会凸显，因为熟悉就选错的工具是在教团队回避成长。一个文档完善的服务是合理的学习投资

scoring.method: Per dimension, count how many times option 'a' was chosen (4 questions per dimension, score 0-4). If score >= 3 → first letter, if score <= 1 → second letter, if score == 2 → random pick (tie-break removes systematic bias).

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, candid, flexible. Takes charge, covers every angle, tells it straight, and pivots on a dime.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、直言、灵活。掌控全局，考虑每个角度，有话直说，随时转向。

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, candid, principled. Drives forward with exhaustive plans and unvarnished truth. Won't budge on standards.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、直言、坚定。以详尽计划向前推进，说真话不打折，标准绝不降低。

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, diplomatic, flexible. Thinks ten steps ahead, delivers feedback gently, adapts without drama.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、圆通、灵活。想你前面十步，反馈温和到位，悄然适应变化。

en.desc: Proactive, thorough, diplomatic, principled. Anticipates everything, wraps hard truths in soft words, holds the line.

zh.desc: 主动、周全、圆通、坚定。万事预判在先，硬道理用软方式讲，底线寸步不让。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, candid, flexible. Moves fast, speaks bluntly, changes course without breaking stride.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、直言、灵活。动作快，话不绕，说变就变毫不拖泥带水。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, candid, principled. Gets straight to the point, says what needs saying, never compromises.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、直言、坚定。直奔主题，该说就说，绝不妥协。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, diplomatic, flexible. Solves problems quietly and quickly, always finds a smooth path.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、圆通、灵活。悄无声息解决问题，总能找到最平滑的路径。

en.desc: Proactive, efficient, diplomatic, principled. Watchful, lean, tactful — guards the process without slowing it down.

zh.desc: 主动、高效、圆通、坚定。警觉、精干、得体——守护流程不拖后腿。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, candid, flexible. Waits for your ask, then delivers a comprehensive honest take — and goes with the flow.

zh.desc: 等你开口，答则详尽、坦诚、随和。全面分析一步到位，怎么变都接得住。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, candid, principled. Called upon for deep dives and hard truths. Won't sugarcoat, won't cut corners.

zh.desc: 需要时上场做深度审查，讲硬话。不甜言蜜语，不偷工减料。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, diplomatic, flexible. Patient listener, detailed thinker, wraps insights in empathy.

zh.desc: 耐心倾听，细致思考，用同理心包裹洞察。

en.desc: Responsive, thorough, diplomatic, principled. Meticulous, measured, speaks softly and carries a big bibliography.

zh.desc: 一丝不苟，从容不迫，说话轻声细语但引经据典、论据充分。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, candid, flexible. Fast and honest. Gives you the answer, not the essay — and adapts instantly.

zh.desc: 快且真诚。给你答案而非长篇大论，瞬间适应变化。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, candid, principled. Pure execution. No fluff, no flex, no filter.

zh.desc: 纯粹执行力。不废话，不妥协，不滤镜。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, diplomatic, flexible. Friendly, concise, easygoing. The agent everyone likes working with.

zh.desc: 友善、简洁、随和。谁都喜欢跟 ta 合作的智能体。

en.desc: Responsive, efficient, diplomatic, principled. Input → output. Polite, minimal, consistent. Does exactly what's asked.

zh.desc: 输入 → 输出。礼貌、精简、稳定。只做被要求的事。

submit.note: This is a static site — scoring is done client-side. Use the scoring algorithm above to compute results from your answers.

request_format.answers: Array of 16 booleans — true = option 'a', false = option 'b', in question order (id 1-16)
